title: betula pendula f. viscosa
intro: Betula pendula f. viscosa, also known as the sticky birch, is a variant of the silver birch tree (Betula pendula) characterized by its sticky sap. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Betula pendula f. viscosa include reducing fever, treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne, and alleviating symptoms of the common cold. In herbal preparations, the sticky sap can be used to make salves, ointments, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs like honey and calendula for enhanced efficacy.
